# Hair Salon / Barbing Salon Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-hair-salon-hair-salon-site  
**Vertical slug:** hair-salon  
**Niche family:** NF-COM-BEA (standalone)  
**Family role:** standalone  
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's hair care market is one of the largest in Africa, estimated at over ₦2 trillion annually. The country has over 2 million registered and informal hair salons and barbing shops — from roadside barbers in Agbado to high-end Lekki Phase 1 studios. Nigerian women spend heavily on hair: braiding (kiko, box braids, Ghana weave), relaxers, weaves, wigs, and treatments. Men's barbing shops are on every street corner. Most Nigerian salons rely on WhatsApp for appointments, Instagram for marketing, and word-of-mouth for trust. Very few have professional websites — huge digital gap.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
**Persona A — Bimpe Adeyemi, 28, Lekki**: Works in marketing, visits a mid-range Lekki salon every 3 weeks for weave installation or knotless braids. Googles salons by area, checks Instagram, books via WhatsApp. Wants to see a price list, waiting time, and photos before calling.

**Persona B — Emeka Okonkwo, 24, Enugu**: University graduate, visits a barber every 2 weeks for low cut / taper fade. Discovered his barber on Instagram but wishes there was a proper site with wait time and booking.

**Persona C — Mama Ngozi, 52, Aba**: Owns a relaxer salon in Aba main market. Wants a website to attract estate clients and corporate walk-ins from nearby firms, having seen competitors in Lagos do the same.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
**Home:** Hero (salon name, tagline, primary CTA "Book via WhatsApp"), service menu preview (braiding, weave, cuts, treatments with NGN prices), trust strip (years in business, client count, NAFDAC products used), about excerpt, gallery CTA, contact strip.
**About:** Salon story, stylists/barbers team, products used (L'Oréal, Dark & Lovely, SoftSheen-Carson), location + opening hours.
**Services:** Full price list in ₦ (braiding ₦3,000–₦25,000; weave ₦8,000–₦50,000; relaxer ₦5,000–₦15,000; men's cut ₦500–₦3,000; kids ₦500–₦2,000).
**Contact:** WhatsApp booking primary, location/map, opening hours, NDPR contact form.

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Warm, confident, beauty-forward. Language: everyday Nigerian English. Avoid: "hair studio" jargon that sounds foreign. Use: "We slay here", "Fresh cuts, fresh vibes", "Your hair, our passion." Primary CTA: "Book Your Appointment" via WhatsApp. Secondary: "See Our Work" (gallery link). AVOID: stock images of Western hair types (straight blonde); use African hair textures only.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
NAFDAC-approved products badge; years in business; client testimonials (Adaeze, Funmi, Chinyere names); stylist certifications (BBTV, Vidal Sassoon Nigeria); SON-compliant equipment; CAC registration if formal business; Instagram follower count as social proof; before/after gallery; accepted payments: bank transfer, POS on-site, Paystack; waiting time indicator.

## 6. Image Direction
1. Nigerian woman sitting in salon chair getting knotless braids installed — close up on hands and hair. Lagos studio lighting. Warm tones.
2. Barber giving a taper fade to a young man in a modern Abuja barbing shop. Clean white walls. Clean tools.
3. Finished hairstyle — full-length Ghana weave with beads — Nigerian woman smiling, colourful background.
4. Display of NAFDAC-approved hair products on salon shelves — Dark & Lovely, ORS, Organic Root Stimulator bottles.
5. Group of stylists in salon uniform (aprons), Lekki or Victoria Island salon interior, professional lighting.
6. Kids' section — child getting cornrows by a female stylist. Gentle, safe environment.
7. Before/after split — natural hair → finished style on dark-skinned Nigerian woman.

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
NAFDAC-approved products only (display "We use NAFDAC-approved products"). CAC registration for formal salons. No MDCN required. NDPR: contact forms need consent. Cosmetic product claims must not be medicinal (SON advisory).

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| WhatsApp CTA home + contact | ✅ REQUIRED |
| All prices in ₦ | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Nigerian cities in examples | ✅ Lekki, Aba, Enugu, Ikeja |
| Nigerian names in testimonials | ✅ Adaeze, Funmi, Bimpe, Emeka |
| NAFDAC trust signal | ✅ Products badge |
| Paystack / bank transfer / POS | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Mobile-first 375px | ✅ REQUIRED |
| esc() on all strings | ✅ REQUIRED |
| NDPR consent form | ✅ REQUIRED |
